channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Webb Space Telescope Reveals Pluto's Moon Charon Composition
item.description: Pluto's moon Charon composition and evolution revealed by James Webb Space Telescope observations
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYT Strands today — hints, answers and spangram for Wednesday, October 2 (game #213)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Inside HHS’ ‘one-stop shop’ for health sector cybersecurity
item.description: In the wake of the Change Healthcare ransomware attack, HHS has been expanding its "one-stop shop" for working with the healthcare sector on cyber issues. The post Inside HHS’ ‘one-stop shop’ for health sector cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Fiscal Service Explores Future of Automation with AI and APIs
item.description: Federal agency looks to AI and API integration for enhanced operations
item.dc:creator: Dana Sukontarak, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal Agencies Embracing Cloud Cost Transparency: A Path to Efficiency
item.description: Federal agencies are adopting cloud cost transparency practices, leveraging FinOps and TBM to optimize cloud utilization.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat has failed to act on rampant reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Epic Games is suing Google and Samsung for this phone setting
item.description: Video game maker Epic Games sued Google and Samsung on Monday, accusing the tech companies of coordinating to block third-party competition in application distribution on Samsung devices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: WWW creator Tim Berners-Lee closes down his foundation
item.description: The World Wide Web Foundation, the organization whose mission has been to make the web safer and more accessible, has shut down, according to The Register. The foundation, which close its virtual doors Sept.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft Copilot gets Voice and Vision features
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday announced several updates to its free Microsoft Copilot and paid-for Copilot Pro services aimed at making the personal AI assistant more powerful and easier to converse with. Among the updates is Copilot Vision.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Drone delivery service Wing teams with Serve to expand delivery in Dallas
item.description: Serve Robotics, which makes autonomous sidewalk delivery bots, and Wing, Alphabet’s on-demand drone delivery service, announced a pilot partnership on Tuesday meant to expand autonomous last-mile food delivery in Dallas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Donald Trump’s crypto venture begins sign-ups while business purpose remains unclear
item.description: World Liberty is just the latest of Trump’s crypto efforts. The Republican presidential nominee has made supporting crypto a part of his platform as he seeks reelection in November.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Judge partially dismisses FTC's antitrust lawsuit against Amazon
item.description: A federal judge on Monday partially dismissed the Federal Trade Commission’s (FTC) antitrust lawsuit against Amazon. However, few details are currently available about U.S. District Judge John Chun’s decision, which was filed under seal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Does your Halloween rager really need a Partiful invite?
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: ONCD’s Harry Wingo on lowering barriers to good-paying cyber jobs
item.description: The Office of the National Cyber Director wants to fill thousands of open federal jobs under its Service for America initiative. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: ONCD’s Harry Wingo on lowering barriers to good-paying cyber jobs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5M over robotaxi crash reporting
item.description: General Motors's autonomous vehicle unit, Cruise, will pay a $1.5 million fine after it failed to disclose details initially about a serious crash involving a pedestrian last year, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Rushing for AI ROI? Chances are it will cost you
item.description: Many organizations have struggled to find the ROI after launching AI projects, but there’s a danger in demanding too much too soon, according to IT research and advisory firm Forrester.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Solving IT’s identity crisis
item.description: Identity is a surprisingly complex issue in today’s world. Battles over political identities, confusion and activism regarding gender identities, and a surfeit of identity crises dominate headlines and social discourse.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: Meteorologists calculate that more than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stuck NASA astronauts will now have a ride home on a SpaceX capsule
item.description: The two astronauts stuck at the International Space Station since June welcomed their new ride home with Sunday’s arrival of a SpaceX capsule.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Arkansas Sues YouTube Over Alleged Addiction and Mental Health Crisis Among Youth
item.description: State officials claim video-sharing platform is deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among young people in Arkansas
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The iPhone 16 Pro Max is a bigger upgrade than I was expecting
item.description: My iPhone 13 Pro Max was working just fine when I decided to upgrade to the latest model. The 13 Pro Max still feels fast, takes excellent photos, and easily lasts all day on a full charge even at 88% battery health.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Intel launches Xeon 6 processors and Gaudi 3 AI accelerators
item.description: Intel has formally launched its next Xeon 6 server processors as well as the Gaudi 3 AI accelerators, making some pretty big boasts in the process.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Exxon Mobil says advanced recycling is the answer to plastic waste. But is it really?
item.description: Despite its seemingly eco-friendly name, the attorney general’s lawsuit denounced advanced recycling as a “public relations stunt” that largely involves superheating plastics to convert them into fuel.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Think twice before using public computers: Here's why
item.description: Using public computers poses security risks, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says, so take precautions or use your own secure device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crypto Owners Split Down the Middle: Harris and Trump in Deadlock
item.description: New polling reveals that American crypto owners are evenly split between supporting Vice President Harris and former President Trump in November's election, reflecting a significant voting bloc with implications for the electoral cycle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft gives enterprises new reasons to adopt Fabric
item.description: Microsoft is working to make its cloud-based Fabric combined offering of data warehousing, data science, data engineering, and data analytics services more attractive, previewing a host of new features at its first annual European Microsoft Fabric Community Conference last week.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese EV tech ban not an overreaction: National security experts
item.description: A tech ban on EVs will limit the Chinese government from accessing data on American drivers, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI bad actors 'not very Christian'
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video game actors' union calls for strike against 'League of Legends'
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — “League of Legends” is caught in the middle of a dispute between Hollywood’s actors union and an audio company that provides voiceover services for the blockbuster online multiplayer game.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5
